wiki.summary: "It’s a song about meeting God. I remember looking at a bunch of people’s Myspace when I first saw Myspace and the ‘Who I’d Like To Meet’ the #1 thing people picked was God. I think about that. I don’t know how many times I’ve experienced something where I felt like God showed up and saved me or loved me for no reason sometimes. That’s why I sing this song, I sing it to God. It’s really intimate as well, you can think about it as a relationship between two people in love, too. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "It’s a song about meeting God. I remember looking at a bunch of people’s Myspace when I first saw Myspace and the ‘Who I’d Like To Meet’ the #1 thing people picked was God. I think about that. I don’t know how many times I’ve experienced something where I felt like God showed up and saved me or loved me for no reason sometimes. That’s why I sing this song, I sing it to God. It’s really intimate as well, you can think about it as a relationship between two people in love, too. I never think about it that way, but what’s so cool about that is I think there’s parallels everywhere – in everything in life. I think that that parallel is something that God wants to communicate to us, that he gives us relationships like that, an intimacy like that." – Lacey Sturm on the radio show LA Lloyd’s Rock 30 Read more on Last.fm.
